title: Atomic Habits
excerpt: A practical framework for building good habits and breaking bad ones through the power of small, incremental changes that compound into remarkable results.
keyIdeas: The four laws of behavior change: make it obvious, attractive, easy, satisfying
Habits are the compound interest of self-improvement
keyTakeaways: Small changes (1% improvements) compound into remarkable results over time
The most effective way to change habits is to focus on who you wish to become, not what you wish to achieve
Habit stacking: use an existing habit as a trigger for a new one
The environment is more important than motivation — design your environment for success
whoShouldRead: Anyone wanting to build lasting habits
People who struggle with consistency and follow-through
Readers of The Power of Habit who want a practical how-to guide
whoShouldSkip: Those looking for quick fixes or 30-day challenges
People who prefer theoretical depth over practical frameworks
